meta.title: Archive
meta.description: Displays a filterable archive of posts from a custom post type with taxonomy filtering, search, pagination, and per-page controls.

requiredPatterns.usage: SSR preview of archive results in editor
Editable heading area with eyebrow, heading, description, buttons
Sync block name in list view with heading content

componentStructure.headingArea: ThemeHeading (2-column layout with heading left, description+buttons right)

qualityChecklist: TSX and PHP heading/layout structure match
No hardcoded colors - uses theme tokens
Card template parts exist for all configured post types
AJAX filtering with URL state management
Dark mode section styling per project pattern
Accent color CSS custom properties applied per card

variations.name: post
variations.title: Blog Archive
variations.description: Displays a filterable archive of blog posts
variations.notes: Blog posts use alternating card layouts (image-top default, text-top alternate). Each card's accent color is set by the post's category accent_color term meta. The 'Filter by Date' dropdown and 'Filter by Topic' dropdown are shown. A 'EXPLORE MORE STUDENT VOICES' button is present in the ThemeHeading area.

variations.name: team_member
variations.title: Team Members Archive
variations.description: Displays a filterable archive of team members
variations.notes: Team member cards have a bright accent color background (blue in the design). The card shows: photo (rounded corners, inset within card), department tag, name (first_name + last_name from meta), role (from excerpt), and 'GET IN TOUCH' link. Filter is 'FILTER BY DEPARTMENT' but team_member has no taxonomies registered - this is flagged as an unresolved decision. A 'LEARN MORE' button appears in ThemeHeading.

variations.name: resource
variations.title: Resources Archive
variations.description: Displays a filterable archive of resources
variations.notes: Resource cards are dark background (charcoal) with accent-colored border on hover. Card shows: multiple taxonomy tags (pills), title, excerpt, related resource type tags, and 'VIEW RESOURCE' link. The Figma shows 'FILTER BY AUDIENCE' and 'FILTER BY RESOURCE TYPE' dropdowns but resource has no taxonomies registered - flagged as unresolved.

variations.name: testimonial
variations.title: Testimonials Archive
variations.notes: Testimonial is a PRIVATE CPT (public: false, no archive, not publicly queryable). Uses meta fields: name, message. No Figma design available. Card should display the message as a quote and the name as attribution. This variation will not have filters. The archive block must use WP_Query directly (not global query) since testimonial has no public archive.

variations.name: policy
variations.title: Policies Archive
variations.description: Displays a filterable archive of policies
variations.notes: Policy cards are dark charcoal background with accent-colored border and neon-green fill on hover. Card shows: category tags (POLICIES & BYLAWS, RESOURCE TYPE), title, and 'VIEW RESOURCE' link. The policies page uses 'FILTER BY TOPIC' dropdown. Currently no taxonomies registered for policy - flagged as unresolved.

attributes.name: blockVariation
attributes.description: Determines which post type the archive queries

attributes.name: eyebrow
attributes.description: Eyebrow text above the heading (not visible in any of the Figma designs but included for ThemeHeading consistency)

attributes.name: heading
attributes.description: Main heading text. Examples: 'Students in Their Own Words', 'We're Here to Help!', 'IGNITE Is Accountable to You, the Student.'

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: Description text displayed alongside the heading (right column on desktop)

attributes.name: buttons
attributes.description: CTA buttons displayed in the heading area. Team Members shows 'LEARN MORE', Blog shows 'EXPLORE MORE STUDENT VOICES'

attributes.name: displaySearch
attributes.description: Show/hide keyword search input. Default false because only the Blog variation shows search in the designs.

attributes.name: displayPerPage
attributes.description: Show/hide the per-page selector (SHOW [9] PER PAGE). Visible in all desktop Figma designs.

attributes.name: postsPerPage
attributes.description: Default number of posts to display per page. All Figma designs show 9 as default.

attributes.name: maxColumns
attributes.description: Maximum number of grid columns. All Figma designs show 3 columns.

attributes.name: paginationMode
attributes.description: Pagination mode. All Figma designs show 'LOAD MORE' button, so default is load-more.

section.notes: All archive designs show dark mode (charcoal/black background). Uses the project dark mode pattern with transparent section bg.

darkModeWrapper.classes: relative before:absolute before:bg-charcoal before:rounded-3xl before:-z-1 before:-inset-(--side-gutter) md:before:-inset-8
darkModeWrapper.notes: Project dark mode pattern: pseudo-element charcoal card. Wraps ThemeHeading + archive-inner.

themeHeading.notes: 2-column layout: heading left, description + buttons right. Heading uses text-header-2 (Anton font). Buttons are secondary variant (outline). All designs show this 2-column pattern.

form.notes: Contains filter dropdowns, search (if enabled), clear all, and per-page selector

filterSelect.notes: Each taxonomy dropdown wrapper. Uses the global select styles (rounded-full, border-current, etc.)

perPageSelector.notes: Right-aligned. Contains 'SHOW' label, select dropdown for count, and 'PER PAGE' label.

select.notes: Small rounded-full select with page count options

searchInput.notes: Only shown for blog variation. Contains search icon + input + Search button.

activeFilters.notes: Filter pills with clear-all button. Hidden when only clear-all button exists (no active filters).

filterPill.classes: inline-flex items-center gap-1 border border-current rounded-full px-3 py-1 text-body-small uppercase font-medium tracking-wider
filterPill.notes: Active filter pill with remove X icon

resultsGrid.classes: grid grid-cols-1 gap-x-10 gap-y-12 sm:grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3
resultsGrid.notes: Responsive grid based on maxColumns attribute. gap-x-10 (40px) and gap-y-12 (48px) match Figma measurements.

loadMoreButton.notes: Centered below grid. Uses secondary button style (outline, rounded-full). Figma shows this as outlined dark button.

post.layout: Two alternating layouts: default (image-top) and alternate (text-top, image-bottom). Cards alternate via CSS :nth-child(even). Both layouts are the same card element, just with flex direction reversed.

article.notes: Alternate cards use flex-col-reverse for odd items via :nth-child CSS

imageContainer.notes: Image uses custom clip-path (notched corners like a ticket). Approx 281x333px in Figma.

image.notes: Clip-path creates notched/ticket corners. On hover, the border/outline becomes the accent color.

topicTag.classes: inline-block bg-accent-light text-charcoal text-body-small uppercase font-medium tracking-wider px-2 py-1.5 rounded-full w-fit
topicTag.notes: Uses the category's accent_color term meta to set --accent-color CSS custom property on the card wrapper. The tag background uses bg-accent-light.

button.notes: 'LEARN MORE' with arrow icon. Uses btn-tertiary style. On hover, text becomes accent/orange color (from Figma hover state 811:59803).

post.accentColorIntegration: Each post card wrapper gets a CSS class matching the category's accent_color term meta (e.g., 'neon-green', 'blue', 'purple'). This sets --accent-color, --accent-color-light, --accent-color-lighter CSS custom properties. The topic tag uses bg-accent-light, and the hover state uses accent colors for the border/outline and the 'LEARN MORE' text.

hoverState.description: On hover, the 'LEARN MORE' text changes to the accent color (orange in the design). The card image border/clip-path outline also gets an accent color tint.

alternateLayout.description: Alternate cards show text/tag/title/date ABOVE the image instead of below. This creates a visual masonry-like stagger in the 3-column grid. Implemented via CSS :nth-child(even) with flex-col-reverse, or via nth-child alternation on the card class.

teamMember.layout: Card with bright accent-color background (bg-accent), photo inset with padding, department tag, name, role, and CTA link.

article.classes: flex flex-col h-full rounded-2xl overflow-hidden bg-accent text-charcoal
article.notes: Bright accent color background. Blue in the Figma design, but should use bg-accent so it responds to the section's accent color. Border-radius ~16px.

imageContainer.notes: Photo has padding on sides and top (16px). The image itself has rounded corners.

image.notes: Approx 395x317px in Figma. Uses photo meta field (attachment ID). Rounded corners ~12px.

contentArea.notes: Padding 24px sides/bottom, 16px top (24px gap from image)

departmentTag.classes: inline-block bg-charcoal/10 text-charcoal text-body-small uppercase font-medium tracking-wider px-2 py-1.5 rounded-full w-fit
departmentTag.notes: Semi-transparent dark background on the accent color. Shows department name if taxonomy exists.

name.notes: Constructed from first_name + last_name meta fields. Anton font.

role.notes: From the post excerpt. Anton font italic style shown in Figma.

ctaLink.notes: 'GET IN TOUCH' with arrow icon. Uses btn-tertiary style. Text is charcoal (on accent bg).

teamMember.notes: The team member card uses the 'photo' meta field (integer, attachment ID) for the image, NOT the featured image/thumbnail. Name is constructed from first_name and last_name meta. Role comes from the excerpt.

resource.layout: Dark charcoal card with accent-colored border, taxonomy tags, title, excerpt, related tags, and CTA link.

article.classes: flex flex-col h-full rounded-2xl border border-white/20 bg-charcoal p-6 group-hover:bg-accent-lighter group-hover:border-accent group-hover:text-charcoal transition-colors
article.notes: Dark card with subtle border. On hover, background becomes accent-lighter and text stays charcoal. Approx 416x341px in Figma.

taxonomyTags.notes: Multiple taxonomy tags (RESOURCE TYPE, AUDIENCE). Each tag is a pill with accent-light bg.

tag.classes: inline-block bg-accent-light text-charcoal text-body-small uppercase font-medium tracking-wider px-2 py-1.5 rounded-full

relatedTags.classes: flex flex-wrap gap-1 text-body-small uppercase font-medium tracking-wider mb-4
relatedTags.notes: Additional resource type tags shown as plain text labels. Only visible in hover/expanded view per Figma.

ctaLink.notes: 'VIEW RESOURCE' with arrow. On hover state, text becomes charcoal (on accent-lighter bg).

hoverState.description: On hover, the card background changes to accent-lighter (neon-green-lighter), border becomes accent color, and all text becomes charcoal. The card expands to show the excerpt and related tags.

policy.layout: Dark charcoal card (simpler than resource), with category tags, title, and CTA link. Accent fill on hover.

article.classes: flex flex-col h-full rounded-2xl border border-white/20 bg-charcoal p-6 group-hover:bg-accent group-hover:border-accent group-hover:text-charcoal transition-colors
article.notes: Dark card. On hover, fills with accent color (neon-green). Approx 416x206px.

tag.classes: inline-block bg-accent-light text-charcoal text-body-small uppercase font-medium tracking-wider px-2 py-1.5 rounded-full

title.notes: Anton font. Policy names like 'IGNITE By-Laws'.

hoverState.description: On hover, the entire card background becomes accent color (neon-green) and all text/elements become charcoal.

testimonial.layout: Quote card for testimonials. No Figma design provided. Will follow project visual patterns.

wrapper.notes: No link since testimonials are private and not publicly queryable

article.classes: flex flex-col h-full rounded-2xl border border-white/20 bg-charcoal p-6
article.notes: Matches the dark card pattern from resource/policy cards

message.notes: The testimonial message from meta. Displayed as a quote.

name.classes: text-body-small uppercase font-medium tracking-wider not-italic mt-auto
name.notes: The testimonial author name from meta

testimonial.notes: Testimonial is a private CPT with no single page. Cards do not link anywhere. Design is inferred from the dark card pattern used by policy/resource cards since no Figma design exists.

behavior.heading: ThemeHeading 2-column layout
behavior.filters: Inline filter dropdowns with per-page right-aligned

emptyState.noResults: Show 'No results found' message centered in results area
emptyState.noFilters: If displayFilters is false and no taxonomies configured, hide entire filter bar
emptyState.loadMore: Hide Load More button when on last page

accentColorPerCard.post: Each blog post card reads the category's accent_color term meta and applies the corresponding CSS class (neon-green, blue, purple, etc.) to the card wrapper. This sets --accent-color custom properties for that card.
accentColorPerCard.teamMember: Uses the section/page accent color (from body class). All team member cards share the same accent color.

dateFilter.description: The blog variation shows a 'FILTER BY DATE' dropdown. This should use archive year/month grouping. Implemented as a custom dropdown that filters by year or year-month.
dateFilter.implementation: Generate options from published post dates. On selection, add date query parameters.

perPageSelector.description: All variations show 'SHOW [9] PER PAGE'. The dropdown allows users to change the number of posts displayed. Options: 9, 18, 27, 36 (multiples of grid columns).
perPageSelector.implementation: A select dropdown that sets the posts_per_page query var. AJAX-reloads results on change.

blogCardAlternation.description: Blog post cards alternate between image-top (default) and text-top (alternate) layouts. This creates visual variety in the grid.
blogCardAlternation.implementation: Use CSS :nth-child(even) on the card article to apply flex-col-reverse, or pass an index to the card template to toggle the layout.

clipPathImage.description: Blog post card images use a custom clip-path that creates notched corners (ticket-style). This is a decorative clip-path applied via CSS.
clipPathImage.implementation: Define a clip-path polygon in CSS on the image container. The notched corners are small triangular cuts at the corners.

loadMore.ariaLive: polite on results container for AJAX updates

cards.notes: Each card is an <a> wrapping an <article>. The link provides the interactive target.

decisions.decision: All archive variations use dark mode (charcoal background) with the project's dark mode pseudo-element pattern
decisions.evidence: All four Figma desktop designs (811:59920, 811:59618, 811:60477, 811:58898) show dark/charcoal backgrounds

decisions.decision: ThemeHeading uses 2-column layout with heading left, description+buttons right
decisions.evidence: All desktop designs show heading on left, description text and button on right side

decisions.decision: Default grid is 3 columns on desktop
decisions.evidence: All four desktop designs show a 3-column grid of cards

decisions.decision: Blog post cards use alternating image-top/text-top layouts via :nth-child
decisions.evidence: Figma node 811:59920 (blog) shows alternating card layouts. Node 811:59752 is image-top default, node 811:59707 is text-top alternate.

decisions.decision: Blog post card accent color comes from category's accent_color term meta
decisions.evidence: Spec states: 'In the post post type, each card has its accent color set by the post_cat taxonomy color.'

decisions.decision: Team member cards use accent color background (bg-accent)
decisions.evidence: Figma node 811:58898 shows team member cards with bright blue background. The blue matches the --color-blue token. Using bg-accent allows it to respond to section accent color.

decisions.decision: Resource and policy cards are dark charcoal with accent-colored hover states
decisions.evidence: Resource default (811:60587) and policy default (811:60010) show dark cards. Resource hover (811:60521) shows accent-lighter bg. Policy hover (811:59964) shows accent fill.

decisions.decision: Default pagination mode is 'load-more'
decisions.evidence: All four desktop designs show a 'LOAD MORE' button at the bottom, not standard pagination numbers

decisions.decision: Per-page selector with 'SHOW [9] PER PAGE' is displayed right-aligned in filter bar
decisions.evidence: All desktop designs (811:59920, 811:59618, 811:60477, 811:58898) show 'SHOW 9 PER PAGE' right-aligned

decisions.decision: Filter dropdowns and Clear All are left-aligned, per-page selector is right-aligned in the same row
decisions.evidence: All desktop designs show filters left, per-page right in a single row

decisions.decision: Heading uses text-header-2 (Anton font, 48px mobile / 60px desktop)
decisions.evidence: All desktop headings use large Anton font consistent with text-header-2

decisions.decision: Blog variation shows both date filter and topic filter, plus search
decisions.evidence: Figma 811:59920 shows 'FILTER BY DATE', 'FILTER BY TOPIC' dropdowns with a topic checklist dropdown, and search functionality. Mobile design 811:60400 also shows date/topic filters.

decisions.decision: Blog topic filter uses a checkbox-list dropdown (multi-select)
decisions.evidence: Figma 811:59920 shows a dropdown with checkboxes: ALL, ADVOCACY, FINANCIAL WELLBEING, GOVERNANCE, MEMBERSHIP, MENTAL WELLBEING, SOCIAL WELLBEING

decisions.decision: Team member card photo uses the 'photo' meta field, not featured image
decisions.evidence: Orchestrator notes state team_member has meta: first_name, last_name, photo (integer attachment ID)

decisions.decision: Team member name is constructed from first_name + last_name meta fields
decisions.evidence: Orchestrator notes state team_member has meta: first_name, last_name. Figma shows 'Name & Last Name'.

decisions.decision: Testimonial cards do not link (testimonial is a private CPT, not publicly queryable)
decisions.evidence: Orchestrator notes: 'Testimonial is a PRIVATE CPT (public: false, no archive, not publicly queryable)'. Post type registration confirms public: false, publicly_queryable: false.

decisions.decision: Blog ThemeHeading includes a button (e.g., 'EXPLORE MORE STUDENT VOICES'). Team Members heading also includes a button ('LEARN MORE'). Buttons use secondary variation.
decisions.evidence: Blog desktop (811:59920) shows 'EXPLORE MORE STUDENT VOICES' button. Team Members desktop (811:58898) shows 'LEARN MORE' button. Both are outline/secondary style.

decisions.decision: Blog post card image uses a clip-path with notched corners
decisions.evidence: Figma nodes 811:59752, 811:59707, 811:59803 all show images with small triangular notches cut from the corners, creating a ticket-like shape

decisions.decision: Tags/pills use bg-accent-light with charcoal text across all card types
decisions.evidence: All card designs show neon-green/light-accent colored pills for category/type tags

decisions.decision: CTA links on cards use btn-tertiary style with arrow icon (uppercase text + arrow SVG)
decisions.evidence: All card designs show 'LEARN MORE', 'VIEW RESOURCE', 'GET IN TOUCH' with right-pointing arrow

unresolvedDecisions.question: The Team Members Figma shows a 'FILTER BY DEPARTMENT' dropdown, but team_member post type has no taxonomies registered. Should a 'department' taxonomy be created for team_member?
options: Remove the department filter from the team_member variation (displayFilters: false)
unresolvedDecisions.defaultIfNotAsked: Create a 'department' taxonomy for team_member, since the Figma explicitly shows it
unresolvedDecisions.reason: Figma design shows department filter, but no taxonomy exists in the codebase

unresolvedDecisions.question: The Resources Figma shows 'FILTER BY AUDIENCE' and 'FILTER BY RESOURCE TYPE' dropdowns, but resource post type has no taxonomies registered. Should 'resource_audience' and 'resource_type' taxonomies be created?
options: Create both 'resource_audience' and 'resource_type' taxonomies for resource
Remove filters from the resource variation (displayFilters: false)
unresolvedDecisions.defaultIfNotAsked: Create both taxonomies since the Figma explicitly shows them
unresolvedDecisions.reason: Figma design shows two filter dropdowns, but no taxonomies exist in the codebase

unresolvedDecisions.question: The Policies Figma shows a 'FILTER BY TOPIC' dropdown, but policy post type has no taxonomies registered. Should a 'policy_topic' taxonomy be created for policy?
options: Remove filter from the policy variation (displayFilters: false)
unresolvedDecisions.defaultIfNotAsked: Create a 'policy_topic' taxonomy for policy, since the Figma explicitly shows it
unresolvedDecisions.reason: Figma design shows topic filter, but no taxonomy exists in the codebase

unresolvedDecisions.question: The blog cards show a 'date' filter (FILTER BY DATE). How should date filtering be implemented?
unresolvedDecisions.defaultIfNotAsked: Year-based dropdown derived from published post dates
unresolvedDecisions.reason: Figma shows 'FILTER BY DATE' but does not specify the date format or granularity

unresolvedDecisions.question: The blog variation shows a checkbox-list dropdown for topic filtering (multi-select with checkboxes visible). Should this use the standard hidden-input + select pattern from the block-type doc, or a custom checkbox-list dropdown component?
options: Standard hidden-input + select multi-select pattern (simpler, consistent with block-type doc)
Custom checkbox-list dropdown matching the Figma design exactly
unresolvedDecisions.defaultIfNotAsked: Custom checkbox-list dropdown matching the Figma design, since the checkbox UI is explicitly shown in the blog desktop design
unresolvedDecisions.reason: Figma 811:59920 explicitly shows a dropdown with checkboxes (ALL, ADVOCACY, etc.), which is a different UI pattern than a standard select

unresolvedDecisions.question: The blog mobile design shows 'Explore more student voices' as a regular text link (not a button), while desktop shows it as a secondary button. Is this intentional, or should mobile also show the button style?
options: Mobile shows btn-tertiary text link, desktop shows btn-secondary button
unresolvedDecisions.defaultIfNotAsked: Mobile shows btn-secondary (same as desktop) since ThemeHeading handles button rendering consistently
unresolvedDecisions.reason: The mobile Figma may show a simplified version. ThemeHeading renders buttons consistently across breakpoints.

variations.name: Blog Archive - Full
variations.description: Blog archive with all features enabled

attributes.heading: Students in Their Own Words
attributes.description: Have a burning question, comment, or concern? Connect with one of our staff members.

variations.name: Team Members Archive - Full
variations.description: Team members grid with department filter

attributes.heading: We're Here to Help!
attributes.description: Have a burning question, comment, or concern? Connect with one of our staff members. From career success to wellness, we're ready to support you.

variations.name: Resources Archive - Full
variations.description: Resources grid with audience and type filters

attributes.heading: Find Resources That Support You
attributes.description: Have a burning question, comment, or concern? Connect with one of our staff members. From career success to wellness, we're ready to support you.

attributes.heading: IGNITE Is Accountable to You, the Student.
attributes.description: You can find a collection of IGNITE's policies and financial statements, as well as a schedule of board meetings for the upcoming year, and executive summaries of previous meetings.

variations.name: Blog Archive - Minimal
variations.description: Blog with no filters, no search, no per-page

variations.name: Blog Archive - Pagination Mode
variations.description: Blog with standard pagination instead of load-more

implementationNotes.fileStructure: This block follows the post-type-archive block type pattern. The block name is 'Archive' (not 'PostTypeArchive') per the user spec.
implementationNotes.cssClassName: Use 'archive' as the base CSS class (not 'post-type-archive') to match the block name. All JS selectors should use '.archive' prefix.
implementationNotes.taxonomyQueryVars: Each taxonomy slug needs to be registered as a query var. This depends on UNRES-001/002/003 resolution.
implementationNotes.clipPathForBlogCards: Blog post images use a clip-path polygon with notched corners. The clip-path should be defined in the Archive.css file as a reusable class.
implementationNotes.arrowIcon: CTA links use an arrow SVG inline. This can be a small inline SVG or a CSS pseudo-element. Figma shows a right-pointing arrow (->).
implementationNotes.perPageImplementation: The per-page selector is a <select> dropdown that triggers an AJAX reload with the new posts_per_page value. The selected value should be persisted in the URL query string.
implementationNotes.dateFilterImplementation: For the blog date filter, query available years from published posts using $wpdb or get_posts. Render as a select dropdown that adds 'year' query parameter.
implementationNotes.darkModeSection: Use the project dark mode pattern: section gets 'dark bg-transparent!' classes, content wrapper gets the pseudo-element charcoal background.
implementationNotes.headingButtonVariation: ThemeHeading buttons should use 'secondary' variation (outline button). The Figma shows outline-style buttons in the heading area.

questionsForUser: UNRES-001: Should a 'department' taxonomy be created for team_member? (Figma shows 'FILTER BY DEPARTMENT' but no taxonomy exists)
UNRES-002: Should 'resource_audience' and 'resource_type' taxonomies be created for resource? (Figma shows both filters)
UNRES-003: Should a 'policy_topic' taxonomy be created for policy? (Figma shows 'FILTER BY TOPIC')
UNRES-004: What date format for blog date filter? (Year-based, year-month, or date range?)
UNRES-005: Should topic filter use standard select or custom checkbox-list dropdown as shown in Figma?
UNRES-006: Mobile button style for ThemeHeading - match desktop (btn-secondary) or use text link?
